channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – July 20, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here.
item.source:markdown: _The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here._

item.title: Worth the Fare: Fresh loaves, delicious pastries at Jyan Isaac Bread
item.description: It is often said that the secret to the famous New York City bagel is in the water, but at Jyan Isaac Bread in Santa Monica, it’s the expertise baked into every loaf.
item.source:markdown: It is often said that the secret to the famous New York City bagel is in the water, but at Jyan Isaac Bread in Santa Monica, it’s the expertise baked into every loaf.

item.title: Film breakdown: Sizing up Serbian standout Nikola Kusturica’s on-court skill set
item.description: This post was updated July 19 at 11:59 p.m. For the first time since 2023, UCLA men’s basketball brought in a five-star freshman recruit, with guard/forward Nikola Kusturica set to join the Bruins ahead of the 2026-27 season from FC Barcelona Bàsquet.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 19 at 11:59 p.m._ For the first time since 2023, UCLA men’s basketball brought in a five-star freshman recruit, with guard/forward Nikola Kusturica set to join the Bruins ahead of the 2026-27 season from FC Barcelona Bàsquet.

item.title: 2026 Big Ten Preview: Washington
item.description: This post was updated July 20 at 12:06 p.m. UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any other Big Ten school.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 20 at 12:06 p.m._ UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any other Big Ten school.

item.title: Opinion: Finding an internship feels impossible. UCLA must fix that
item.description: The smell of chlorine hits my nose before the water does, waking me up for my morning shift at the pool. I’m a lifeguard in my hometown.
item.source:markdown: The smell of chlorine hits my nose before the water does, waking me up for my morning shift at the pool. I’m a lifeguard in my hometown.

item.title: North Westwood Neighborhood Council recap – July 1
item.description: The North Westwood Neighborhood Council elected two board members and passed a community impact statement in support of taxing future Olympic ticket purchases at its July meeting.
item.source:markdown: The North Westwood Neighborhood Council elected two board members and passed a community impact statement in support of taxing future Olympic ticket purchases at its July meeting.

item.title: Film review: Christopher Nolan’s new film is an ‘Odyssey’ worth the adventure
item.description: A man. A thought. A war. In a time of apparent magic, “The Odyssey” proves the ancient tale is still worth retelling. Christopher Nolan’s 2026 action-adventure blockbuster adapts the millennia-old Homeric poem like never before.
item.source:markdown: A man. A thought. A war. In a time of apparent magic, “The Odyssey” proves the ancient tale is still worth retelling. Christopher Nolan’s 2026 action-adventure blockbuster adapts the millennia-old Homeric poem like never before.

item.title: UC regents discuss $350 million funding increase, cuts to scholarships and programs
item.description: This post was updated July 20 at 12:30 p.m. While Gov. Gavin Newsom increased the UC’s state funding for the upcoming fiscal year, the approved budget also included cuts to academic programs and scholarships, the UC Board of Regents said.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 20 at 12:30 p.m._ While Gov. Gavin Newsom increased the UC’s state funding for the upcoming fiscal year, the approved budget also included cuts to academic programs and scholarships, the UC Board of Regents said.

item.title: Album review: Gracie Abrams wrestles with growing up in ‘Daughter from Hell’
item.description: This post was updated July 19 at 8:05 p.m. Gracie Abrams grew up in Hollywood, and still can’t decide if that’s a confession or an excuse.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 19 at 8:05 p.m._ Gracie Abrams grew up in Hollywood, and still can’t decide if that’s a confession or an excuse.

item.title: UCLA women’s water polo coach Adam Wright steps down, remains men’s coach
item.description: This post was updated July 19 at 11:27 p.m. After stepping away from day-to-day operations as head coach of the women’s water polo team in December due to rehabilitation from back and nerve pain, Adam Wright has officially resigned from the position, UCLA Athletics announced Friday.  Wright will continue to coach the men’s squad – a position he has held since 2009.  [Related: Women’s water polo coach Adam Wright to step away from day-to-day duties] UCLA will begin a national search for its next head coach.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 19 at 11:27 p.m._ After stepping away from day-to-day operations as head coach of the women’s water polo team in December due to rehabilitation from back and nerve pain, Adam Wright has officially resigned from the position, UCLA Athletics announced Friday. Wright will continue to coach the men’s squad – a position he has held since 2009. \[Related: Women’s water polo coach Adam Wright to step away from day-to-day duties\] UCLA will begin a national search for its next head coach.

item.title: UC Regents hear public comment on SAT, ACT debate, undocumented student concerns
item.description: This post was updated July 20 at 12:28 a.m. Faculty and students called on the UC Board of Regents to reinstate standardized testing requirements and support undocumented students at its July meeting.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 20 at 12:28 a.m._ Faculty and students called on the UC Board of Regents to reinstate standardized testing requirements and support undocumented students at its July meeting.

item.title: Restaurant review: Worth the wait? Raising Cane’s Westwood location packs flavor into every dollar
item.description: This post was updated July 19 at 7:51 p.m. For three years, Bruins walked past construction barriers. Now, they’re walking out with Box Combos. As of Monday, Raising Cane’s Chicken Fingers, located on the corner of Broxton Avenue and Weyburn Avenue, is open for business.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 19 at 7:51 p.m._ For three years, Bruins walked past construction barriers. Now, they’re walking out with Box Combos. As of Monday, Raising Cane’s Chicken Fingers, located on the corner of Broxton Avenue and Weyburn Avenue, is open for business.

item.title: Argentina rallied to beat England 2-1 to reach the World Cup Final
item.description: England and Argentina fought for the last remaining spot in the FIFA World Cup final. Argentina made yet another comeback against England in the closing minutes to clinch a 2-1...
item.source:markdown: England and Argentina fought for the last remaining spot in the FIFA World Cup final. Argentina made yet another comeback against England in the closing minutes to clinch a 2-1...

item.title: 21.com and the Case for a More Mature Casino Marketing Voice
item.description: In many ways, online casino marketing has been based on noise. The strategy of how many brands attempt to grab attention has been influenced by big welcome offers, urgent language, flashing promotions and countless claims of being the best online casino.
item.source:markdown: In many ways, online casino marketing has been based on noise. The strategy of how many brands attempt to grab attention has been influenced by big welcome offers, urgent language, flashing promotions and countless claims of being the best online casino.

item.title: Gallery: Everything’s Bigger in Texas, including World Cup celebrations
item.description: FIFA Fan Festival Dallas continued at Fair Park on Tuesday, where fans flocked to watch the FIFA World Cup semifinal match between Spain and France, which took place in Arlington, Texas.
item.source:markdown: FIFA Fan Festival Dallas continued at Fair Park on Tuesday, where fans flocked to watch the FIFA World Cup semifinal match between Spain and France, which took place in Arlington, Texas.

item.title: Mobilizer, connector, spectator, or outsider? New Pew research categorizes how Americans do (or don’t) engage civically
item.description: Civic engagement takes many forms. The term describes activities as disparate as voting, volunteering, attending religious services, and following the news. Yet “these behaviors are often studied separately, making it difficult to see how they come together in people’s day-to-day lives,” researchers from the Pew-Knight Initiative write in a new report published Thursday. Their study,...
item.source:markdown: Civic engagement takes many forms. The term describes activities as disparate as voting, volunteering, attending religious services, and following the news. Yet “these behaviors are often studied separately, making it difficult to see how they come together in people’s day-to-day lives,” researchers from the Pew-Knight Initiative write in a new report published Thursday. Their study,...

item.title: Looking back at FAST’s annual runway show
item.description: On May 22, UCLA’s student fashion club, FAST (Fashion and Student Trends), hosted their annual runway show. The Daily Bruin looks back at the designs and shares the visions of some of the designers in this retrospective.
item.source:markdown: On May 22, UCLA’s student fashion club, FAST (Fashion and Student Trends), hosted their annual runway show. The Daily Bruin looks back at the designs and shares the visions of some of the designers in this retrospective.

item.title: Want to build audience and revenue through live events? These five news nonprofits have some ideas to get you started
item.description: PITTSBURGH — One indicator of the energy and interest around live events in newsrooms these days: At INN Days — the Institute for Nonprofit News’ annual conference, held last month in Pittsburgh — a series of lightning talks on “Events that Build Audience and Revenue” was a standing-room-only session. Presenters from five nonprofit newsrooms described...
item.source:markdown: PITTSBURGH — One indicator of the energy and interest around live events in newsrooms these days: At INN Days — the Institute for Nonprofit News’ annual conference, held last month in Pittsburgh — a series of lightning talks on “Events that Build Audience and Revenue” was a standing-room-only session. Presenters from five nonprofit newsrooms described...

item.title: Pro Bruin Rundown: Bruin volleyball, water polo stars shine on the international stage
item.description: This post was updated July 19 at 11:53 p.m. As UCLA Athletics takes a break for the summer, professional sports are still in full swing, with many Bruins, past and present, representing their teams.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 19 at 11:53 p.m._ As UCLA Athletics takes a break for the summer, professional sports are still in full swing, with many Bruins, past and present, representing their teams.

item.title: Lauren Betts furthers Bruin legacy with historic ESPY victory
item.description: This post was updated July 15 at 6:07 p.m. The 2025-26 UCLA women’s basketball squad has not slowed down in its pursuit of making history. Former UCLA center Lauren Betts won the ESPY for Best College Athlete – Women’s Sports, beating out Pittsburgh volleyball’s Olivia Babcock, Northwestern lacrosse’s Madison Taylor and Oklahoma gymnastics’ Faith Torrez on Wednesday night.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 15 at 6:07 p.m._ The 2025-26 UCLA women’s basketball squad has not slowed down in its pursuit of making history. Former UCLA center Lauren Betts won the ESPY for Best College Athlete – Women’s Sports, beating out Pittsburgh volleyball’s Olivia Babcock, Northwestern lacrosse’s Madison Taylor and Oklahoma gymnastics’ Faith Torrez on Wednesday night.

item.title: USAC recap – July 7
item.description: The Undergraduate Students Association Council approved its 2026-27 budget and allocated $3,038.90 from its discretionary fund to a non-USAC entity at its July 7 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.
item.source:markdown: The Undergraduate Students Association Council approved its 2026-27 budget and allocated $3,038.90 from its discretionary fund to a non-USAC entity at its July 7 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.

item.title: At Kesem at UCLA, children impacted by family cancer discover community, connection
item.description: This post was updated July 20 at 12:33 a.m. A free, student-run summer camp is fostering community and support for children who have lived with a parent or caregiver with cancer.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 20 at 12:33 a.m._ A free, student-run summer camp is fostering community and support for children who have lived with a parent or caregiver with cancer.

item.title: Spain Beats Star-Led France 2-0 to Advance to World Cup Final
item.description: Two European soccer teams battled for a spot in the FIFA World Cup final. France has been led by forward Kylian Mbappe, who has scored a tournament-leading eight goals, along...
item.source:markdown: Two European soccer teams battled for a spot in the FIFA World Cup final. France has been led by forward Kylian Mbappe, who has scored a tournament-leading eight goals, along...

item.title: Business Insider’s big bet against aggregation
item.description: “It’s not: Some people do features, some people do spot stories. You’re commanding your beat. You’re doing everything,” said Jamie Heller, Business Insider’s editor-in-chief. It’s a Tuesday morning news meeting at Business Insider’s headquarters in New York’s Financial District. More than a dozen editors have gathered in the conference room, while several more chime in...
item.source:markdown: “It’s not: Some people do features, some people do spot stories. You’re commanding your beat. You’re doing everything,” said Jamie Heller, Business Insider’s editor-in-chief. It’s a Tuesday morning news meeting at Business Insider’s headquarters in New York’s Financial District. More than a dozen editors have gathered in the conference room, while several more chime in...

item.title: Gallery: LACMA, Jeffrey Deitch host 1st-ever Los Angeles Art Parade on Wilshire Boulevard
item.description: The Los Angeles County Museum of Art has declared art is off the walls.  The first-ever Los Angeles Art Parade took place along Wilshire Boulevard’s Museum Row on June 20.
item.source:markdown: The Los Angeles County Museum of Art has declared art is off the walls. The first-ever Los Angeles Art Parade took place along Wilshire Boulevard’s Museum Row on June 20.

item.title: UC Academic Senate to decide on recommending SAT, ACT reinstatement by June 2027
item.description: The UC Academic Senate will decide whether or not to recommend reinstating standardized testing requirements for undergraduate admissions by June 2027, the UC Board of Regents announced Tuesday.  UC Regents Chair Maria Anguiano announced the decision during its July meeting at UC San Francisco.
item.source:markdown: The UC Academic Senate will decide whether or not to recommend reinstating standardized testing requirements for undergraduate admissions by June 2027, the UC Board of Regents announced Tuesday. UC Regents Chair Maria Anguiano announced the decision during its July meeting at UC San Francisco.

item.title: Gallery: BelicoFest makes California debut at BMO Stadium, celebrates música mexicana
item.description: BelicoFest set foot in California with headliner Tito Double P at BMO Stadium on Sunday night. For The Culture co-founders Ramiro Bojorquez and Andrés Charvel – the latter of whom is responsible for promoting some of Shakira’s Mexico performances – brought rising regional Mexican talent to the United States with the first BelicoFest in Arizona in 2024.
item.source:markdown: BelicoFest set foot in California with headliner Tito Double P at BMO Stadium on Sunday night. For The Culture co-founders Ramiro Bojorquez and Andrés Charvel – the latter of whom is responsible for promoting some of Shakira’s Mexico performances – brought rising regional Mexican talent to the United States with the first BelicoFest in Arizona in 2024.

item.title: Opinion: America at 250: Celebrating our diverse culture, reflecting on our shared identity
item.description: Monumental birthdays are cause for grand celebrations, but they are also ripe for reflection. Every milestone brings about renewed hope for the future and insight into the past.
item.source:markdown: Monumental birthdays are cause for grand celebrations, but they are also ripe for reflection. Every milestone brings about renewed hope for the future and insight into the past.

item.title: Jordan Roiland named 2027-28 UC student regent, first medical student to serve
item.description: This post was updated July 14 at 2:01 p.m. The UC Board of Regents appointed Jordan Roiland to be the 2027-28 student regent Tuesday.  Roiland, who is the first medical student to serve in the role, earned a bachelor’s degree in applied mathematics & statistics and biochemistry from Stony Brook University and earned a Master of Science in Health and Medical Sciences from UC Berkeley in 2025.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 14 at 2:01 p.m._ The UC Board of Regents appointed Jordan Roiland to be the 2027-28 student regent Tuesday. Roiland, who is the first medical student to serve in the role, earned a bachelor’s degree in applied mathematics & statistics and biochemistry from Stony Brook University and earned a Master of Science in Health and Medical Sciences from UC Berkeley in 2025.

item.title: One name at a time: How Die Zeit built a searchable database of Nazi Party members
item.description: In the final days of World War II, as the German Reich collapsed, Nazi officials ordered millions of party membership cards to be destroyed. The vast card index that documented membership across Germany survived largely because a paper mill operator, Hanns Huber, chose to hand the records over to the advancing US forces rather than...
item.source:markdown: In the final days of World War II, as the German Reich collapsed, Nazi officials ordered millions of party membership cards to be destroyed. The vast card index that documented membership across Germany survived largely because a paper mill operator, Hanns Huber, chose to hand the records over to the advancing US forces rather than...

item.title: In Ethiopia, fact-checking can be a matter of life and death
item.description: As reports of drone strikes and fresh clashes between Ethiopian government forces and Tigrayan fighters surfaced in late January, fears of a return to full-scale war spread quickly. Flights to northern Ethiopia were cancelled, and tensions rose ahead of the national elections in June, bringing renewed attention to the role misinformation played during the last conflict. In...
item.source:markdown: As reports of drone strikes and fresh clashes between Ethiopian government forces and Tigrayan fighters surfaced in late January, fears of a return to full-scale war spread quickly. Flights to northern Ethiopia were cancelled, and tensions rose ahead of the national elections in June, bringing renewed attention to the role misinformation played during the last conflict. In...

item.title: Capital B is ready to open a third local news operation — about two hours east of its first one
item.description: The Black-focused national-and-local news outlet Capital B launched in 2022 with a staff of 16 and a first local focus on Atlanta, with plans to expand. Co-founders Lauren Williams and Akoto Ofori-Atta — then editor-in-chief of Vox and managing editor of The Trace, respectively — were looking for “large Black cities that have especially bad...
item.source:markdown: The Black-focused national-and-local news outlet Capital B launched in 2022 with a staff of 16 and a first local focus on Atlanta, with plans to expand. Co-founders Lauren Williams and Akoto Ofori-Atta — then editor-in-chief of Vox and managing editor of The Trace, respectively — were looking for “large Black cities that have especially bad...

item.title: TV preview: Summer shows bring action, book adaptations to the small screen
item.description: This post was updated July 15 at 1:01 p.m. The small screen is set to stay sizzling all summer. Clear skies and sunshine promise a slate of returning and premiering shows, some newly released and some upcoming, ranging from frontier journeys to female-centered crime thrillers.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 15 at 1:01 p.m._ The small screen is set to stay sizzling all summer. Clear skies and sunshine promise a slate of returning and premiering shows, some newly released and some upcoming, ranging from frontier journeys to female-centered crime thrillers.

item.title: Lab coats provide safety at a steep cost. Friends, student organizations can help
item.description: Each year, thousands of students must buy lab coats and protective gear to complete their major requirements. Students said there are many ways to forgo the expensive costs of lab equipment, from borrowing from friends to accessing student-run lab coat rental programs.
item.source:markdown: Each year, thousands of students must buy lab coats and protective gear to complete their major requirements. Students said there are many ways to forgo the expensive costs of lab equipment, from borrowing from friends to accessing student-run lab coat rental programs.

item.title: UC board rescinds original timeline to reconsider SAT, ACT in admissions
item.description: A UC faculty board decided Friday to indefinitely repeal its original timeline for considering if the University should use standardized testing in first-year undergraduate admissions.  The UC Board of Admissions and Relations announced in June that it would establish two work groups during the 2026-27 academic year to consider reinstating SAT and ACT requirements, after hundreds of faculty called on the University to bring back the tests.
item.source:markdown: A UC faculty board decided Friday to indefinitely repeal its original timeline for considering if the University should use standardized testing in first-year undergraduate admissions. The UC Board of Admissions and Relations announced in June that it would establish two work groups during the 2026-27 academic year to consider reinstating SAT and ACT requirements, after hundreds of faculty called on the University to bring back the tests.

item.title: Opinion: In the wake of Boyle Heights fire, ‘environmental justice is everyone’s problem’
item.description: Black smoke billowed. The stench of rotting food permeated the neighborhood. This is not a scene from a science fiction novel. It is the reality of the seven-day Lineage warehouse fire in Boyle Heights, which began June 17.
item.source:markdown: Black smoke billowed. The stench of rotting food permeated the neighborhood. This is not a scene from a science fiction novel. It is the reality of the seven-day Lineage warehouse fire in Boyle Heights, which began June 17.

item.title: Teamsters Local 2010 strikes five-year agreement with UC, increasing minimum wage
item.description: This post was updated July 13 at 4:43 p.m. A union representing administrative professionals and clerical workers across the UC voted to approve a new contract with the University July 2.  Teamsters Local 2010’s Clerical & Allied Services CX unit – which represents 22,000 UC workers – reached the five-year agreement after 10 months of bargaining and with 99% of voting members in favor, according to a July 2 press release from the union.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 13 at 4:43 p.m._ A union representing administrative professionals and clerical workers across the UC voted to approve a new contract with the University July 2. Teamsters Local 2010’s Clerical & Allied Services CX unit – which represents 22,000 UC workers – reached the five-year agreement after 10 months of bargaining and with 99% of voting members in favor, according to a July 2 press release from the union.

item.title: Raising Cane’s opens Westwood location after three-year wait
item.description: Raising Cane’s opened in Westwood on Monday after a three-year wait. The Louisiana-based fried chicken restaurant held its grand opening on the corner of Broxton Avenue and Weyburn Avenue at 8 a.m., featuring a ribbon-cutting ceremony, giveaways, a live DJ, face painting and a caricature artist.
item.source:markdown: Raising Cane’s opened in Westwood on Monday after a three-year wait. The Louisiana-based fried chicken restaurant held its grand opening on the corner of Broxton Avenue and Weyburn Avenue at 8 a.m., featuring a ribbon-cutting ceremony, giveaways, a live DJ, face painting and a caricature artist.

item.title: Local news went all in on the World Cup. Here’s what worked.
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted in North America this summer, has been fascinating to watch from different angles — from the matches themselves to community celebrations to the controversies. The tournament has brought hundreds of thousands of visitors to major cities in the United States, Canada, and Mexico. It’s been directing new and returning...
item.source:markdown: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted in North America this summer, has been fascinating to watch from different angles — from the matches themselves to community celebrations to the controversies. The tournament has brought hundreds of thousands of visitors to major cities in the United States, Canada, and Mexico. It’s been directing new and returning...

item.title: This app turns the web into a scrollable feed (just don’t call it RSS)
item.description: As an elder millennial who still mourns the death of Google Reader (13 years ago this month, sigh) and uses the Feedly daily, I was intrigued by TechCrunch’s post: “A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed.” HyperTexting, launched on iOS on June 29, says its goal is “to...
item.source:markdown: As an elder millennial who still mourns the death of Google Reader (13 years ago this month, sigh) and uses the Feedly daily, I was intrigued by TechCrunch’s post: “A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed.” HyperTexting, launched on iOS on June 29, says its goal is “to...

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – July 13, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here.
item.source:markdown: _The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here._

item.title: 6 Bruins selected on Day 2 of 2026 MLB Draft
item.source:markdown: After four Bruins were selected on the first day of the 2026 MLB Draft, six more Bruins were selected on the second and final day – which consisted of rounds five through 20. Right-hander Cal Randall, outfielder Will Gasparino, outfielder Dean West, catcher Cashel Dugger, right-hander Michael Barnett and right-hander Justin Lee were all selected by MLB clubs Sunday. Cal Randall: St.

item.title: 2026 Big Ten Preview: Michigan State
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 10:28 p.m. UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 10:28 p.m._ UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.

item.title: Editorial: California’s legislature must not make public records harder to access
item.description: Last August, the Daily Bruin Editorial Board called on UCLA to comply with the California Public Records Act, a law that gives citizens access to state and local government records.
item.source:markdown: Last August, the Daily Bruin Editorial Board called on UCLA to comply with the California Public Records Act, a law that gives citizens access to state and local government records.

item.title: Amid student concerns, Bruin One Access to increase to $149 per quarter
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 7:42 p.m. UCLA is raising the quarterly fee for its digital textbook program, which students said could deter them from using the service.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 7:42 p.m._ UCLA is raising the quarterly fee for its digital textbook program, which students said could deter them from using the service.

item.title: Will Gasparino goes No. 161 in MLB draft for Phillies
item.description: One of the Bruins’ best home-run hitters is on his way to the next level. Center fielder Will Gasparino was selected by the Philadelphia Phillies in the fifth round with the No.
item.source:markdown: One of the Bruins’ best home-run hitters is on his way to the next level. Center fielder Will Gasparino was selected by the Philadelphia Phillies in the fifth round with the No.

item.title: Album review: Future falls short in truly unmasking himself in ‘The Real Me’
item.description: The ever-elusive Pluto remains inscrutable for a different reason: there’s not much left to uncover. “The Real Me” is Future’s tenth studio album, released on Friday, and arrives just two years after his legendary three-album run, which featured two collaborative albums with producer Metro Boomin and the solo mixtape “MIXTAPE PLUTO.” That 2024 stretch marked one of the most prolific periods in his career, earning him four Grammy nominations and chart-topping songs such as “Like That,” which peaked at No.
item.source:markdown: The ever-elusive Pluto remains inscrutable for a different reason: there’s not much left to uncover. “The Real Me” is Future’s tenth studio album, released on Friday, and arrives just two years after his legendary three-album run, which featured two collaborative albums with producer Metro Boomin and the solo mixtape “MIXTAPE PLUTO.” That 2024 stretch marked one of the most prolific periods in his career, earning him four Grammy nominations and chart-topping songs such as “Like That,” which peaked at No.

item.title: Roman Martin goes No. 111 in MLB Draft to Athletics
item.description: The Bruin who manned the hot corner for the last two seasons is on his way to play professional baseball.  Third baseman Roman Martin was selected by the Athletics in the fourth round with the No.
item.source:markdown: The Bruin who manned the hot corner for the last two seasons is on his way to play professional baseball. Third baseman Roman Martin was selected by the Athletics in the fourth round with the No.

item.title: Mulivai Levu goes No. 70 in MLB draft for Reds
item.description: The Bruins’ RBI machine is on his way to play professional baseball. First baseman Mulivai Levu was selected by the Cincinnati Reds in Competitive Balance Round B with the No.
item.source:markdown: The Bruins’ RBI machine is on his way to play professional baseball. First baseman Mulivai Levu was selected by the Cincinnati Reds in Competitive Balance Round B with the No.

item.title: Logan Reddemann goes No. 38 in MLB draft for Rockies
item.source:markdown: Right-hander Logan Reddemann was selected with the 38th pick in the second round of the 2026 MLB draft by the Colorado Rockies on Saturday. The junior exploded onto the draft scene after transferring to UCLA from San Diego, where he played his first two seasons of collegiate baseball and was selected to the All-WCC First Team twice. Reddemann captured the Bruins’ Friday night starter role before the season began.

item.title: Roch Cholowsky goes No. 1 in MLB draft for White Sox
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 10:19 p.m. UCLA baseball already had a shortstop wearing jersey No. 1 when Roch Cholowsky arrived in Westwood as a freshman in the fall of 2023.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 10:19 p.m._ UCLA baseball already had a shortstop wearing jersey No. 1 when Roch Cholowsky arrived in Westwood as a freshman in the fall of 2023.

item.title: Dizon’s Disposition: UCLA baseball draft profiles 2026: part 3
item.description: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft will take place Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.
item.source:markdown: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft will take place Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.

item.title: Dizon’s Disposition: UCLA baseball draft profiles 2026: part 2
item.description: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft will take place Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.
item.source:markdown: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft will take place Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.

item.title: How the Trump administration’s revamp of funding guidelines could threaten research
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 7:39 p.m. The federal government’s proposal to overhaul research grant funding processes could have detrimental effects on scientific research, UCLA researchers said.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 7:39 p.m._ The federal government’s proposal to overhaul research grant funding processes could have detrimental effects on scientific research, UCLA researchers said.

item.title: Life sciences department may force botanical club to vacate greenhouse space
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 7:37 p.m. UCLA’s life sciences department is pressuring a student-run botanical club to vacate an educational greenhouse it has used for the past two years, students said.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 7:37 p.m._ UCLA’s life sciences department is pressuring a student-run botanical club to vacate an educational greenhouse it has used for the past two years, students said.

item.title: ‘Creative process builds community’: New UCLA fellowship to use arts to combat burnout
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 7:51 p.m. A new UCLA fellowship will provide arts-based mental health workshops for healthcare workers and civic leaders experiencing stress and burnout.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 7:51 p.m._ A new UCLA fellowship will provide arts-based mental health workshops for healthcare workers and civic leaders experiencing stress and burnout.

item.title: Pro Bruin Rundown: UCLA men’s basketball stars take on international league, NBA
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 10:30 p.m. As UCLA Athletics takes a break for the summer, professional sports are still in full swing, with many Bruins, past and present, representing their teams.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 10:30 p.m._ As UCLA Athletics takes a break for the summer, professional sports are still in full swing, with many Bruins, past and present, representing their teams.

item.title: Local Los Angeles golf club and France USA Club host World Cup watch party
item.description: The Penmar Golf Club and France USA Club hosted a watch party for the France versus Morocco World Cup match, bringing both fan bases together as France earned a 2-0...
item.source:markdown: The Penmar Golf Club and France USA Club hosted a watch party for the France versus Morocco World Cup match, bringing both fan bases together as France earned a 2-0...

item.title: Dizon’s Disposition: UCLA baseball draft profiles 2026: part 1
item.description: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft spans Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.
item.source:markdown: The 20-round 2026 MLB Draft spans Saturday and Sunday. UCLA baseball boasts 23 players who could be selected in the First-Year Player Draft, meeting the draft’s eligibility requirement of being at least 21 or spending at least three seasons at a four-year university.

item.title: Q&A: Andrés Charvel on BelicoFest’s mission to promote música mexicana in LA and beyond
item.description: This post was updated July 12 at 10:42 p.m. With its Los Angeles expansion, BelicoFest is regional no more. The music festival will make its California debut at BMO Stadium on Sunday with headliner Tito Double P after two years in Phoenix.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 12 at 10:42 p.m._ With its Los Angeles expansion, BelicoFest is regional no more. The music festival will make its California debut at BMO Stadium on Sunday with headliner Tito Double P after two years in Phoenix.

item.title: The New York Times will launch a local newsletter pilot in the Twin Cities next month
item.description: The New York Times: It wants to be your one-stop shop for cooking, games, sports…and next, perhaps, local news? On Wednesday, the Times announced it will soon launch The Local, “a new journalism initiative aimed at serving and engaging communities.” The national news behemoth will kick off the new initiative in August with a pilot...
item.source:markdown: The New York Times: It wants to be your one-stop shop for cooking, games, sports…and next, perhaps, local news? On Wednesday, the Times announced it will soon launch The Local, “a new journalism initiative aimed at serving and engaging communities.” The national news behemoth will kick off the new initiative in August with a pilot...

item.title: There are millions of undiscovered species on Earth. Vox is trying to find one.
item.description: CENTRAL PARK, NEW YORK CITY — On a hot June day in Manhattan, a few minutes away from the sea lion pool in Central Park Zoo, up a hill and through a patch of shoulder-height shrubs, Benji Jones peered into a bottle filled with ethanol and dead bugs. “Oh, there’s some big guys in here,”...
item.source:markdown: CENTRAL PARK, NEW YORK CITY — On a hot June day in Manhattan, a few minutes away from the sea lion pool in Central Park Zoo, up a hill and through a patch of shoulder-height shrubs, Benji Jones peered into a bottle filled with ethanol and dead bugs. “Oh, there’s some big guys in here,”...
item.category: Norwegian University of Science and Technology

item.title: Serbian star Nikola Kusturica joins UCLA men’s basketball roster
item.description: Mick Cronin has his guy. Serbian FC Barcelona Bàsquet wing Nikola Kusturica committed to UCLA men’s basketball Thursday, the California Post reported.
item.source:markdown: Mick Cronin has his guy. Serbian FC Barcelona Bàsquet wing Nikola Kusturica committed to UCLA men’s basketball Thursday, the California Post reported.

item.title: Argentina has faced close games throughout the World Cup
item.description: Egypt appeared to believe they had the game won against Lionel Messi and Argentina, but that was not the case. Egypt was hoping to make history by reaching the quarterfinals...
item.source:markdown: Egypt appeared to believe they had the game won against Lionel Messi and Argentina, but that was not the case. Egypt was hoping to make history by reaching the quarterfinals...

item.title: FoodieLand dishes out diverse foods, serves up a variety of vendors at the Rose Bowl
item.description: A food-centered festival brought thousands of people to the Rose Bowl from July 3-5 to try diverse cuisines. The nation’s largest food festival transformed Pasadena’s Rose Bowl into the ultimate culinary experience.
item.source:markdown: A food-centered festival brought thousands of people to the Rose Bowl from July 3-5 to try diverse cuisines. The nation’s largest food festival transformed Pasadena’s Rose Bowl into the ultimate culinary experience.

item.title: USAC recap – June 23
item.description: The Undergraduate Students Association Council appointed students to the USA judicial board, elections board and USAC committees at its June 23 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.
item.source:markdown: The Undergraduate Students Association Council appointed students to the USA judicial board, elections board and USAC committees at its June 23 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.

item.title: Man sentenced for stealing, replacing rare Chinese manuscripts from UCLA libraries
item.description: A man was sentenced Wednesday for stealing more than $200,000 worth of rare Chinese manuscripts from the UCLA Library system. [Related: Man arrested for allegedly stealing rare Chinese manuscripts from UCLA libraries] Jeffrey Ying, who is from Fremont, California, will serve one year of home confinement and three years on supervised release, according to the San Francisco Chronicle.
item.source:markdown: A man was sentenced Wednesday for stealing more than $200,000 worth of rare Chinese manuscripts from the UCLA Library system. \[Related: Man arrested for allegedly stealing rare Chinese manuscripts from UCLA libraries\] Jeffrey Ying, who is from Fremont, California, will serve one year of home confinement and three years on supervised release, according to the San Francisco Chronicle.

item.title: Driply transforms UCLA’s Chabad House lobby into an unexpected Westwood cafe
item.description: Tucked into the lobby of the Chabad House at UCLA, a Jewish student center in Westwood, is a small shop serving lattes and pour-over coffee. Chabad’s lobby space was largely unused before Driply took it over in October, said Moshe Kashani, the shop’s founder and owner.
item.source:markdown: Tucked into the lobby of the Chabad House at UCLA, a Jewish student center in Westwood, is a small shop serving lattes and pour-over coffee. Chabad’s lobby space was largely unused before Driply took it over in October, said Moshe Kashani, the shop’s founder and owner.

item.title: UCLA cuts 27 DTS jobs amid budget deficit, UPTE-CWA 9119 alleges contract violation
item.description: This post was updated July 9 at 7:08 p.m. UCLA laid off 27 employees in its digital and technology services department and eliminated their positions around late June.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 9 at 7:08 p.m._ UCLA laid off 27 employees in its digital and technology services department and eliminated their positions around late June.

item.title: A new study looks at the skills journalists are losing (and gaining) because of AI tools
item.description: Last year, MIT’s Media Lab came out with a buzzy study on a phenomenon that’s been coined “cognitive debt.” In short, the researchers found that when ChatGPT users offloaded essay writing tasks to their AI assistants, their own writing abilities declined. ChatGPT users consistently had the lowest “brain engagement” and task performance scores across the...
item.source:markdown: Last year, MIT’s Media Lab came out with a buzzy study on a phenomenon that’s been coined “cognitive debt.” In short, the researchers found that when ChatGPT users offloaded essay writing tasks to their AI assistants, their own writing abilities declined. ChatGPT users consistently had the lowest “brain engagement” and task performance scores across the...

item.title: We asked our readers to buy, not donate. Here’s what it taught us about the new math of news.
item.description: Most people in the news business agree on at least one thing right now. As search and AI reshape how people find information, the old model of waiting for audiences to wander in is finished, and we have to build direct relationships with readers instead. The numbers are stark. When Pew Research Center tracked the...
item.source:markdown: Most people in the news business agree on at least one thing right now. As search and AI reshape how people find information, the old model of waiting for audiences to wander in is finished, and we have to build direct relationships with readers instead. The numbers are stark. When Pew Research Center tracked the...

item.title: Classifieds – July 8, 2026
item.description:
The Daily Trojan features Classified advertising in each day's edition. Here you can read, search, and even print out each day's edition of the Classifieds.

The post Classifieds – July 8, 2026 appeared first on Daily Trojan.
item.source:markdown:
The Daily Trojan features Classified advertising in each day's edition. Here you can read, search, and even print out each day's edition of the Classifieds.
The post [Classifieds – July 8, 2026](https://dailytrojan.com/2026/07/08/classifieds-july-8-2026/) appeared first on [Daily Trojan](https://dailytrojan.com).

item.title: From Cholowsky to Reddemann: The Players Defining UCLA Baseball’s Success
item.source:markdown:
The Stars and Strategy Behind One of the Greatest Seasons in UCLA Baseball History A Program Built on Tradition and Talent
Baseball is one of the most storied programs among UCLA’s 11 men’s varsity sports, and the school has long established itself as one of the most competitive in the country. Over the decades, dozens of famous UCLA baseball players have gone on to make their mark at the professional level — but the 2026 season may end up being remembered as one of the program’s finest.

item.title: Rebuild Local News’ Matt Pearce is trying to help journalists and lawmakers speak the same language
item.description: For a growing number of local journalists and supporters of local journalism, the relevant question in 2026 is no longer, “Should public policy to support local news exist?” but “What should public policy to support local news look like here?” At least 21 states and territories have proposed legislation intended to financially support local news...
item.source:markdown: For a growing number of local journalists and supporters of local journalism, the relevant question in 2026 is no longer, “Should public policy to support local news exist?” but “What should public policy to support local news look like here?” At least 21 states and territories have proposed legislation intended to financially support local news...

item.title: The Boston Globe doth return its web-site to yesteryear in celebration of American independence
item.description: Neither The Boston Globe nor the internet existed in the 1700s. But this past holiday weekend, in celebration of the Semiquincentennial (try saying that five times fast), the newspaper took a liberty of its own: imagining what its home page might have looked like on July 4, 1776. The entire page is a sepia-toned delight...
item.source:markdown: Neither The Boston Globe nor the internet existed in the 1700s. But this past holiday weekend, in celebration of the Semiquincentennial (try saying that five times fast), the newspaper took a liberty of its own: imagining what its home page might have looked like on July 4, 1776. The entire page is a sepia-toned delight...

item.title: From UX to Regulation — John Gold’s Takeaways from the iGaming Strategy Conference
item.description: The iGaming Strategy Conference rarely produces consensus. Operators, regulators, platform engineers, and compliance officers occupy the same rooms for three days, and they rarely leave without agreeing on much.
item.source:markdown: The iGaming Strategy Conference rarely produces consensus. Operators, regulators, platform engineers, and compliance officers occupy the same rooms for three days, and they rarely leave without agreeing on much.

item.title: US men’s soccer team falls to Belgium amid FIFA controversy
item.description: The United States men’s soccer team was looking to advance to its first quarterfinal appearance since the 2002 World Cup. They faced a confident Belgium team that came back from...
item.source:markdown: The United States men’s soccer team was looking to advance to its first quarterfinal appearance since the 2002 World Cup. They faced a confident Belgium team that came back from...

item.title: Payton Brennan transfers to Texas after career year with UCLA baseball
item.description: Coach John Savage was able to fend his program from the beast for two-straight offseasons. But Monday, the SEC took a bite out of the Bruins.
item.source:markdown: Coach John Savage was able to fend his program from the beast for two-straight offseasons. But Monday, the SEC took a bite out of the Bruins.

item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here.
item.source:markdown: _The Daily Bruin publishes online issues weekly over the summer. You can view this week’s issue of The Bruin here._

item.title: 2026 Big Ten Preview: Illinois
item.description: UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.
item.source:markdown: UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.

item.title: San Fernando Valley restaurant hosts FIFA World Cup watch party
item.description: Cafe Buenos Aires hosted a watch party for the final day of the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, bringing Argentina fans together to celebrate the team’s nail-biting 3-2 victory...
item.source:markdown: Cafe Buenos Aires hosted a watch party for the final day of the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, bringing Argentina fans together to celebrate the team’s nail-biting 3-2 victory...

item.title: A quiet shift in how Americans perceive local news
item.description: Local news has long been an exception to a troubling trend. Even as trust in national news organizations eroded over the past decade and partisan divides over the media widened, local news largely held its ground — majorities of both Republicans and Democrats continued to rate their local news media favorably. Despite facing major financial...
item.source:markdown: Local news has long been an exception to a troubling trend. Even as trust in national news organizations eroded over the past decade and partisan divides over the media widened, local news largely held its ground — majorities of both Republicans and Democrats continued to rate their local news media favorably. Despite facing major financial...

item.title: The New York Times appears to be cracking down on login sharing
item.description: Disney did it, Netflix is doing it, and now The New York Times is serious about it too: Quit trying to log in with your old coworker’s mom’s email address. Wow, it finally happened. The New York Times cracked down on login sharing and now I can't use my old coworker's mom's account that I've...
item.source:markdown: Disney did it, Netflix is doing it, and now The New York Times is serious about it too: Quit trying to log in with your old coworker’s mom’s email address. Wow, it finally happened. The New York Times cracked down on login sharing and now I can't use my old coworker's mom's account that I've...

item.title: “We cannot be in this journalism silo”: News Futures tries to grow a civic media field
item.description: “We are sitting in the middle of a fucking polycrisis.” That’s how Darryl Holliday introduced the first-ever News Futures Convention in San Francisco in March. While the idea of being in crisis is a common theme of U.S. journalism conferences, this was not a traditional journalism conference. News Futures, fiscally sponsored by the nonprofit civic...
item.source:markdown: “We are sitting in the middle of a fucking polycrisis.” That’s how Darryl Holliday introduced the first-ever News Futures Convention in San Francisco in March. While the idea of being in crisis is a common theme of U.S. journalism conferences, this was not a traditional journalism conference. News Futures, fiscally sponsored by the nonprofit civic...

item.title: Crimewatch: June 2026
item.description: Sources: UCPD. Graphic reporting by Julian Duaybis, Metro editor. Interactive by Bianca Peralta, Data editor. UCPD recorded 554 unique entries in its June 1-30 daily crime log.
item.source:markdown: Sources: UCPD. Graphic reporting by Julian Duaybis, Metro editor. Interactive by Bianca Peralta, Data editor. UCPD recorded 554 unique entries in its June 1-30 daily crime log.

item.title: Gallery: 2026 LA Pride Parade fills Hollywood Boulevard with celebration, community
item.description: For some, June brings sweltering heat. For others, it’s a chance to breathe. The oldest Pride parade in Southern California returned to Hollywood Boulevard on June 14.
item.source:markdown: For some, June brings sweltering heat. For others, it’s a chance to breathe. The oldest Pride parade in Southern California returned to Hollywood Boulevard on June 14.

item.title: Opinion: The internet is a lawless land. We need to change that
item.description: Imagine the internet as a bustling city. There are people, shops, histories and homes, but no city hall or police. Now, let’s say a crime was committed.
item.source:markdown: Imagine the internet as a bustling city. There are people, shops, histories and homes, but no city hall or police. Now, let’s say a crime was committed.

item.title: CJ Weinstein flips from LSU baseball, commits to UCLA
item.source:markdown: UCLA baseball landed its best 2026 recruit yet Sunday. Shortstop CJ Weinstein announced his commitment to UCLA via Instagram on Sunday afternoon, flipping from LSU. Perfect Game’s No.

item.title: Second Take: The World Cup needs Shakira more than Lionel Messi
item.description: The FIFA World Cup has always been as much a spectacle of sound as of sport. That spectacle echoed through Los Angeles when nearly 50,000 fans flooded into Union Station for the Los Angeles World Cup 26 Fan Zone from June 25-28.
item.source:markdown: The FIFA World Cup has always been as much a spectacle of sound as of sport. That spectacle echoed through Los Angeles when nearly 50,000 fans flooded into Union Station for the Los Angeles World Cup 26 Fan Zone from June 25-28.

item.title: SB 79 takes effect, paving the way for more high-density housing in Westwood
item.description: This post was updated July 7 at 5:06 p.m. A California law that took effect Wednesday could bring more high-density housing to Westwood by loosening development restrictions around major transit stops.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 7 at 5:06 p.m._ A California law that took effect Wednesday could bring more high-density housing to Westwood by loosening development restrictions around major transit stops.

item.title: Fault line stress could increase earthquake risk in LA, study shows
item.description: This post was updated July 5 at 11:07 p.m. High stress levels on fault lines under Los Angeles could increase risk of an earthquake in the near future, according to a June study by University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa researchers.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 5 at 11:07 p.m._ High stress levels on fault lines under Los Angeles could increase risk of an earthquake in the near future, according to a June study by University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa researchers.

item.title: 2026 Big Ten Preview: Ohio State
item.description: This post was updated July 6 at 12:19 a.m. UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 6 at 12:19 a.m._ UCLA is set to enter its third season in the Big Ten, following a sophomore campaign in which the program captured three NCAA championships – the most of any Big Ten school.

item.title: Bruin Racing drives innovation, hands-on experience through student-built vehicles
item.description: This post was updated July 5 at 10:47 p.m. UCLA’s largest mechanical engineering organization is working to take car racing and sustainable transportation to the next level.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 5 at 10:47 p.m._ UCLA’s largest mechanical engineering organization is working to take car racing and sustainable transportation to the next level.

item.title: UCLA artist Dea blends storytelling with production-forward rock on first EP
item.description: UCLA student Dea is a new artist in the shoegaze scene, telling stories through cinematic soundscapes, obscured vocals and intentional lyricism. Dice Tam, a rising second-year music industry student, who goes by the artist name Dea, released his first EP “SeeYouSoon” in March 2026.
item.source:markdown: UCLA student Dea is a new artist in the shoegaze scene, telling stories through cinematic soundscapes, obscured vocals and intentional lyricism. Dice Tam, a rising second-year music industry student, who goes by the artist name Dea, released his first EP “SeeYouSoon” in March 2026.

item.title: North Westwood Neighborhood Council recap – June 3
item.description: The North Westwood Neighborhood Council elected a new board member and recommended stricter e-bike use policies at its June 3 meeting. The council, which represents UCLA and Westwood Village, meets on the first Wednesday of every month at UCLA’s Weyburn Terrace.
item.source:markdown: The North Westwood Neighborhood Council elected a new board member and recommended stricter e-bike use policies at its June 3 meeting. The council, which represents UCLA and Westwood Village, meets on the first Wednesday of every month at UCLA’s Weyburn Terrace.

item.title: Opinion: The Iran conflict deserves more attention from informed UCLA students
item.description: This post was updated July 4 at 12:25 a.m. There are two types of UCLA students: those who protest and those who walk by protests. Some feel motivated to speak out.
item.source:markdown: This post was updated July 4 at 12:25 a.m. There are two types of UCLA students: those who protest and those who walk by protests. Some feel motivated to speak out.

item.title: Editorial Cartoon: “Summer Expectations”
$12 billion UC-sponsored research bond misses deadline to appear on ballot
item.description: This post was updated July 5 at 10:18 p.m. A UC-sponsored state bill that would place a $12 billion research bond on the ballot will not appear in the November election unless lawmakers reach an agreement.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 5 at 10:18 p.m._ A UC-sponsored state bill that would place a $12 billion research bond on the ballot will not appear in the November election unless lawmakers reach an agreement.

item.title: Gallery: Next stop: World Cup fever hits Union Station in a sea of jerseys
item.description: The Los Angeles Union Station transformed into a sea of colors Saturday as FIFA World Cup fans wearing national jerseys gathered ahead of the tournament’s final group-phase matches.
item.source:markdown: The Los Angeles Union Station transformed into a sea of colors Saturday as FIFA World Cup fans wearing national jerseys gathered ahead of the tournament’s final group-phase matches.

item.title: From hobby to handmade business: Donna Wu builds Critter Trinkets
item.description: Donna Wu molds her passions into a budding art business. Wu launched her brand Critter Trinkets in October 2025 and sells clay art pieces, ranging from pins and magnets to hair clips, keychains and more.
item.source:markdown: Donna Wu molds her passions into a budding art business. Wu launched her brand Critter Trinkets in October 2025 and sells clay art pieces, ranging from pins and magnets to hair clips, keychains and more.

item.title: Are There Any AI Tools That Can Convert Photos Into Paintings?

item.title: Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought by advancing to the Round of 16

item.title: Film review: ‘Supergirl’ soars with casting, visuals but loses sight of its heroine
item.description: This post was updated July 5 at 10:09 p.m. DC Studios’ new film “Supergirl” finds its kryptonite in its inability to keep the titular hero at the center of her own story.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 5 at 10:09 p.m._ DC Studios’ new film “Supergirl” finds its kryptonite in its inability to keep the titular hero at the center of her own story.

item.title: Are college degrees worth the cost? Study finds UC alumni earn higher wages on average
item.description: This post was updated July 5 at 10:53 p.m. UC undergraduate degrees give students greater financial security and higher earnings compared to non-University degree-holders, according to a June study by the University’s Institutional Research and Academic Planning department.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 5 at 10:53 p.m._ UC undergraduate degrees give students greater financial security and higher earnings compared to non-University degree-holders, according to a June study by the University’s Institutional Research and Academic Planning department.

item.title: Gallery: Savannah Bananas somersault into Autzen Stadium, serving sold-out spectacle in Eugene
item.description: In the most widely broadcasted Banana Ball games to date – and the furthest from their Georgia origins – the Savannah Bananas and Party Animals sold out the University of Oregon’s 60,000-seat Autzen Stadium on Saturday and Sunday.
item.source:markdown: In the most widely broadcasted Banana Ball games to date – and the furthest from their Georgia origins – the Savannah Bananas and Party Animals sold out the University of Oregon’s 60,000-seat Autzen Stadium on Saturday and Sunday.

item.title: The New Immersion Race: How Realistic Worlds Are Changing Viewer Expectations on Twitch and YouTube
item.description: The visual standard that viewers expect from a Twitch or YouTube gaming stream has risen sharply over the past five years. What used to look impressive in a 2019 broadcast...

item.title: Now we’re getting AI fake news complaining about how AI fake news is the death of real news
item.description: Reading sad things about journalism — newspapers, especially — has been a big part of my job for almost two decades. On Monday, I came across such a story that seemed to be getting traction on social media. According to the link aggregator Sill, 18 people I follow on Bluesky had shared a link to...
item.source:markdown: Reading sad things about journalism — newspapers, especially — has been a big part of my job for almost two decades. On Monday, I came across such a story that seemed to be getting traction on social media. According to the link aggregator Sill, 18 people I follow on Bluesky had shared a link to...

item.title: Daily Bruin awarded 4 honors at 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards
item.description: DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES – The Daily Bruin took home four awards at the 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards ceremony Sunday evening. The Los Angeles Press Club hosted the ceremony – held at the Millennium Biltmore Hotel – which honored student media outlets and industry professionals.
item.source:markdown: DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES – The Daily Bruin took home four awards at the 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards ceremony Sunday evening. The Los Angeles Press Club hosted the ceremony – held at the Millennium Biltmore Hotel – which honored student media outlets and industry professionals.

item.title: One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
item.description:
One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
You can spot a good startup-competition judge by one thing. They measure everyone by the same yardstick.
item.source:markdown:
One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
You can spot a good startup-competition judge by one thing. They measure everyone by the same yardstick.

item.title: New faces, fresh hopes: UCLA women’s soccer embraces new era with 5 transfers
item.description: This year, UCLA women’s soccer is all about welcoming new changes. Boasting a new head coach, Gof Boyoko, and two incoming freshmen, and, most notably, five strong transfers, the team has its eyes set on a new road to the NCAA championships.
item.source:markdown: This year, UCLA women’s soccer is all about welcoming new changes. Boasting a new head coach, Gof Boyoko, and two incoming freshmen, and, most notably, five strong transfers, the team has its eyes set on a new road to the NCAA championships.

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom signs state budget, granting UC $350 million funding increase
item.description: This post was updated July 4 at 6:57 p.m. Gov. Gavin Newsom signed California’s state budget for the upcoming fiscal year Monday, increasing the UC’s funding by $350 million.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 4 at 6:57 p.m._ Gov. Gavin Newsom signed California’s state budget for the upcoming fiscal year Monday, increasing the UC’s funding by $350 million.

item.title: Gallery: A celebration of tradition: Root[d brings Indian wedding culture to UCLA
item.description: UCLA Root[d, a South Asian urban fusion dance team, hosted Mock Mehndi on May 20 at the James West Alumni Center. Performers recreated a traditional Indian pre-wedding ceremony at the event.
item.source:markdown: UCLA Root\[d, a South Asian urban fusion dance team, hosted Mock Mehndi on May 20 at the James West Alumni Center. Performers recreated a traditional Indian pre-wedding ceremony at the event.

item.title: Gallery: Daniela Andrade brings intimate storytelling to El Rey Theatre on her “Oda Tour”
item.description: Daniela Andrade is an ode to independent artistry on the “Oda Tour.” The Honduran-Canadian singer-songwriter returned to Los Angeles after nearly a decade for a performance at the El Rey Theatre on Friday.
item.source:markdown: Daniela Andrade is an ode to independent artistry on the “Oda Tour.” The Honduran-Canadian singer-songwriter returned to Los Angeles after nearly a decade for a performance at the El Rey Theatre on Friday.

item.title: Fact-checkers reckon with shrinking budgets and growing AI threats at GlobalFact 2026
item.description: “Welcome to Vilnius, the city where we face cognitive warfare for real,” Robertas Kaunas, Lithuania’s minister of national defense, said in his opening remarks at this year’s GlobalFact. On June 17, hundreds of fact-checkers assembled in Vilnius for the annual conference of the International Fact-Checking Network (IFCN), part of the Poynter Institute. Roughly 180 fact-checking...
item.source:markdown: “Welcome to Vilnius, the city where we face cognitive warfare for real,” Robertas Kaunas, Lithuania’s minister of national defense, said in his opening remarks at this year’s GlobalFact. On June 17, hundreds of fact-checkers assembled in Vilnius for the annual conference of the International Fact-Checking Network (IFCN), part of the Poynter Institute. Roughly 180 fact-checking...

item.title: Opinion: UCLA’s finance alumni network values genuine relationships, not transactions
item.description: At a finance career fair, desperation is easy to spot. Finance has long been an industry in which connections often determine your professional opportunities. UCLA students are usually at an advantage here: They have a vast, engaged network of alumni who want to help fellow Bruins because that identity still means something to them.
item.source:markdown: At a finance career fair, desperation is easy to spot. Finance has long been an industry in which connections often determine your professional opportunities. UCLA students are usually at an advantage here: They have a vast, engaged network of alumni who want to help fellow Bruins because that identity still means something to them.

item.title: What short news videos reveal about the Messi–Ronaldo debate — and news distribution today
item.description: The World Cup is the gift that keeps on giving (to me). I was swiping through Instagram Stories during halftime of the Canada vs. Qatar World Cup match when I saw a post about a study about the political indicators of a person’s preference of soccer stars Lionel Messi, the Argentine forward, or Cristiano Ronaldo,...
item.source:markdown: The World Cup is the gift that keeps on giving (to me). I was swiping through Instagram Stories during halftime of the Canada vs. Qatar World Cup match when I saw a post about a study about the political indicators of a person’s preference of soccer stars Lionel Messi, the Argentine forward, or Cristiano Ronaldo,...

item.title: OPINION: The FIFA World Cup group stage matchups have come to a close
item.description: Over the past few weeks, more than half of the FIFA World Cup matches have been played. With the first round of 32 matchup complete, here are the top five...
item.source:markdown: Over the past few weeks, more than half of the FIFA World Cup matches have been played. With the first round of 32 matchup complete, here are the top five...

item.title: Op-ed: UCLA should prioritize reducing car dependency over expanding parking
item.description: Contrary to the beliefs of the students and faculty interviewed in Avital Abramov’s June 7 piece, “Students, faculty frustrated with high parking costs, limited permit access,” UCLA should not increase parking availability or decrease parking fees.
item.source:markdown: Contrary to the beliefs of the students and faculty interviewed in Avital Abramov’s June 7 piece, “Students, faculty frustrated with high parking costs, limited permit access,” UCLA should not increase parking availability or decrease parking fees.

item.title: Capital Weather is dropping the “Gang” and going independent
item.description: If you have ever lived in the Washington, D.C. metro area, or had the misfortune of being stuck there during a snowstorm, there’s a good chance you’ve heard of the Capital Weather Gang. For eighteen years they were The Washington Post’s oracles of the atmosphere, covering local weather on the Post website and on social...
item.source:markdown: If you have ever lived in the Washington, D.C. metro area, or had the misfortune of being stuck there during a snowstorm, there’s a good chance you’ve heard of the Capital Weather Gang. For eighteen years they were The Washington Post’s oracles of the atmosphere, covering local weather on the Post website and on social...

item.title: With the Expansion of Legalized Online Betting, College Students Face New Risks and Knowledge Gaps
item.description: Online betting is no longer something students encounter occasionally; it has become part of the digital environment many move through every day. As more U.S. states legalize sports betting and online casino platforms, gambling has become easier to access, harder to avoid and more deeply embedded in everyday media.
item.source:markdown: Online betting is no longer something students encounter occasionally; it has become part of the digital environment many move through every day. As more U.S. states legalize sports betting and online casino platforms, gambling has become easier to access, harder to avoid and more deeply embedded in everyday media.

item.title: Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
item.description:
Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
For many student gamers, tapping into a game to claim a daily bonus has become a regular part of campus routines.
item.source:markdown:
Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
For many student gamers, tapping into a game to claim a daily bonus has become a regular part of campus routines.

item.title: Don’t be spooked by this supernatural UCLA course
item.description: Aliens, Psychics and Ghosts – a UCLA course taught by professor David Delgado Shorter – has everything from teacher-led meditations to lively debates about paranormal legends.
item.source:markdown: Aliens, Psychics and Ghosts – a UCLA course taught by professor David Delgado Shorter – has everything from teacher-led meditations to lively debates about paranormal legends.
